text.title: Tool-Call Schema Design Checklist Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules every Tool-Call Schema Design Checklist application MUST enforce. HARD rules — user-supplied input cannot override them.

rule.statement: Tool name is verb-object kebab-case; never bare noun or vague verb.

rationale.source: Tool-Call Schema Design Checklist authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Verb-object names cut hallucinated tool calls; bare nouns get picked up for unrelated jobs.

rule.statement: Description field is written as the prompt the model will read; covers when-to-call AND when-not-to-call.

rationale.source: Tool-Call Schema Design Checklist authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Models route on the description, not on the schema body. Missing when-not-to-call invites tool overuse.

rule.statement: Every categorical argument uses enum; no free-string when the set is closed.

rationale.source: Tool-Call Schema Design Checklist authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Free strings invite typos and synonyms; enums make the call falsifiable at parse time.

rule.statement: Writes carry an idempotency_key argument OR a documented idempotent semantic.

rationale.source: Tool-Call Schema Design Checklist authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Retried calls without idempotency double-bill, double-message, double-create.

rule.statement: Errors return a typed code + remediation hint, not a free-form string.

rationale.source: Tool-Call Schema Design Checklist authoring evidence + websearch 2026-05

rule.rationale: Free-form errors are unactionable by the model; typed errors let the agent self-correct.

section.title: Conclusion targets used by the decision tree

rule.statement: Run the full procedure; emit an artefact passing the output contract.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions hold.

rule.statement: Skip; surface the missing precondition; do not synthesize.
rule.rationale: Terminal target used by 06-decision-tree when preconditions fail.
